what_it_does: This noticeboard item records five architectural facts the author verified before writing a design/code argument, serving as a grounding memo for the board system's storage, identity, edges, and mutation model.
observations: Title labels the item as 'P2 · 0 · The grounding I verified before writing'.
It cites specific source files and line ranges (cc_board.py lines 175-199 and 438-519, address.py, runtime/cognition.py) as evidence.
It asserts storage is Obsidian-shaped: YAML frontmatter plus markdown body, with filename equal to the opaque id.
It highlights nested array-of-object YAML structures (links, history) as the crux of the design question.
It insists identity is flat and opaque: board://<id> where id is 'item-' + uuid hex8, and file path is not identity.
It describes links as typed ADDRESS edges resolved through a single resolver, explicitly not path-based wikilinks.
It states the board is mark-is-truth / append-only: comments/replies/marks file new items; only edit_item/transition mutate in place and append history.
standouts: Identity must be flat and opaque, and file path is explicitly not identity.
Edges are typed ADDRESS edges through one resolver, not wikilinks.
The board is append-only / mark-is-truth, with only two in-place mutators.
Nested YAML frontmatter structures are identified as the central design issue.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item format: YAML frontmatter plus markdown body, opaque id filename.
Uses a typed part_of link to board://item-80c37615.
Uses internal address schemes (board://, decision://, code://, session://) when describing edges.
Section numbering '0 ·' and bullet-heavy argumentative style.
concerns: Specific line-number claims reference external files not included here, so they cannot be verified from this file alone.
Strong normative language ('must', 'categorically NOT', 'the ONLY') is presented as verified fact without showing the verifying evidence.
The 'whole question' and '§4' are alluded to but not stated, leaving the downstream design argument unclear.
notes: A grounding memo that enumerates five verified board-system invariants before advancing a design argument.
questions: What is the parent item-80c37615 and the broader P2 thread this item belongs to?
What is the specific design question in '§4' that makes nested frontmatter the crux?
Are the cited line ranges in cc_board.py, address.py, and runtime/cognition.py still current?
Which design proposal or option set does this grounding precede?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes — the title and body frame the file as a pre-writing grounding checklist, and the content supplies exactly five verified architectural facts.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a shared, evidence-backed baseline of board-system invariants so a subsequent design decision can be argued from agreed premises rather than re-litigating fundamentals.
novelty: It treats a noticeboard item as a self-contained proof-of-grounding, citing live file shapes and specific code line ranges as evidentiary anchors.
contribution: A concise, cited inventory of the board's architectural invariants—storage shape, identity contract, typed address edges, and append-only mutation—that can act as a reference point for design discussions and guard future changes.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item item-37876190 records five verified grounding facts about the board system—Obsidian-shaped markdown storage, nested YAML frontmatter, flat opaque identity, typed address edges, and append-only mark-is-truth mutation—before a design argument.
intention_for_embedding: To establish a shared, evidence-backed baseline of board-system invariants prior to making a design proposal.
